req.title: System-Admin deletes team
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to delete a team through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for requirement:
An authenticated system-admin shall be able to delete a team through the UI form.
Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def system_admin_user():
    """Return a fully-authenticated system-admin user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "usr-001"
    user.username = "admin_user"
    user.role = "system-admin"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def non_admin_user():
    """Return an authenticated user who is NOT a system-admin."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "usr-002"
    user.username = "regular_user"
    user.role = "viewer"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """Return an unauthenticated (anonymous) user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = None
    user.username = None
    user.role = None
    user.is_authenticated = False
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def existing_team():
    """Return a team that exists in the system."""
    team = MagicMock()
    team.id = "team-123"
    team.name = "Alpha Squad"
    team.description = "Core engineering team"
    team.member_count = 5
    return team


@pytest.fixture
def team_repository(existing_team):
    """Return a mock team repository pre-loaded with one team."""
    repo = MagicMock()
    repo.find_by_id.return_value = existing_team
    repo.delete.return_value = True
    repo.exists.return_value = True
    return repo


@pytest.fixture
def audit_service():
    """Return a mock audit service."""
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def delete_team_form_service(team_repository, audit_service):
    """
    Return the service under test, wired with mock dependencies.

    The real implementation is expected to live at:
        app.services.team_service.DeleteTeamFormService
    """
    from app.services.team_service import DeleteTeamFormService  # noqa: PLC0415

    service = DeleteTeamFormService(
        team_repository=team_repository,
        audit_service=audit_service,
    )
    return service


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper / constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD = {
    "team_id": "team-123",
    "confirmation": "DELETE",   # mandatory confirmation token
}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Authentication & authorisation
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestAuthenticationAndAuthorisation:
    """Only an authenticated system-admin may invoke the delete-team form."""

    def test_authenticated_system_admin_can_initiate_delete(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user
    ):
        """Happy path: system-admin with valid payload succeeds."""
        result = delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )
        assert result.success is True

    def test_unauthenticated_user_is_rejected(
        self, delete_team_form_service, unauthenticated_user
    ):
        """Unauthenticated users must receive an authentication error."""
        result = delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=unauthenticated_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )
        assert result.success is False
        assert result.error_code == "NOT_AUTHENTICATED"

    def test_non_admin_authenticated_user_is_rejected(
        self, delete_team_form_service, non_admin_user
    ):
        """A logged-in user without system-admin role must be forbidden."""
        result = delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=non_admin_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )
        assert result.success is False
        assert result.error_code == "FORBIDDEN"

    def test_unauthenticated_user_does_not_trigger_deletion(
        self, delete_team_form_service, unauthenticated_user, team_repository
    ):
        """Repository delete must never be called for unauthenticated actors."""
        delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=unauthenticated_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )
        team_repository.delete.assert_not_called()

    def test_non_admin_user_does_not_trigger_deletion(
        self, delete_team_form_service, non_admin_user, team_repository
    ):
        """Repository delete must never be called for non-admin actors."""
        delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=non_admin_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )
        team_repository.delete.assert_not_called()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Mandatory field validation
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestMandatoryFieldValidation:
    """All mandatory form fields must be present and non-empty."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", ["team_id", "confirmation"])
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_fails_validation(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user, missing_field
    ):
        """Each mandatory field, when absent, must cause a validation failure."""
        payload = dict(VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD)
        del payload[missing_field]

        result = delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=payload,
        )

        assert result.success is False
        assert result.error_code == "VALIDATION_ERROR"
        assert missing_field in result.field_errors

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("empty_value", [None, "", "   "])
    def test_blank_team_id_fails_validation(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user, empty_value
    ):
        """team_id must not be blank or whitespace-only."""
        payload = {**VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD, "team_id": empty_value}

        result = delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=payload,
        )

        assert result.success is False
        assert result.error_code == "VALIDATION_ERROR"
        assert "team_id" in result.field_errors

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("empty_value", [None, "", "   "])
    def test_blank_confirmation_token_fails_validation(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user, empty_value
    ):
        """Confirmation token must not be blank or whitespace-only."""
        payload = {**VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD, "confirmation": empty_value}

        result = delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=payload,
        )

        assert result.success is False
        assert result.error_code == "VALIDATION_ERROR"
        assert "confirmation" in result.field_errors

    def test_wrong_confirmation_token_fails_validation(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user
    ):
        """Confirmation token must equal the expected sentinel value ('DELETE')."""
        payload = {**VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD, "confirmation": "WRONG"}

        result = delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=payload,
        )

        assert result.success is False
        assert result.error_code == "VALIDATION_ERROR"
        assert "confirmation" in result.field_errors

    def test_validation_errors_do_not_trigger_deletion(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user, team_repository
    ):
        """Repository delete must never be called when validation fails."""
        payload = {**VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD, "team_id": ""}

        delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=payload,
        )

        team_repository.delete.assert_not_called()

    def test_validation_errors_do_not_write_audit_record(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user, audit_service
    ):
        """Audit service must not be called when validation fails."""
        payload = {**VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD, "confirmation": "WRONG"}

        delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=payload,
        )

        audit_service.record.assert_not_called()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Business-rule validation
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestBusinessRuleValidation:
    """Domain-level rules beyond simple field presence."""

    def test_deleting_nonexistent_team_returns_not_found(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user, team_repository
    ):
        """Attempting to delete a team that does not exist must fail gracefully."""
        team_repository.find_by_id.return_value = None
        team_repository.exists.return_value = False

        payload = {**VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD, "team_id": "team-999"}

        result = delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=payload,
        )

        assert result.success is False
        assert result.error_code == "TEAM_NOT_FOUND"

    def test_deleting_nonexistent_team_does_not_write_audit(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user, team_repository, audit_service
    ):
        """No audit record must be written when the team is not found."""
        team_repository.find_by_id.return_value = None
        team_repository.exists.return_value = False

        payload = {**VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD, "team_id": "team-999"}
        delete_team_form_service.execute(actor=system_admin_user, payload=payload)

        audit_service.record.assert_not_called()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Successful deletion
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestSuccessfulDeletion:
    """Verify the happy-path behaviour end-to-end."""

    def test_successful_delete_returns_success(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user
    ):
        """execute() must return a successful result on the happy path."""
        result = delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )
        assert result.success is True

    def test_successful_delete_calls_repository_with_correct_id(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user, team_repository
    ):
        """The repository must be called exactly once with the correct team ID."""
        delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )
        team_repository.delete.assert_called_once_with("team-123")

    def test_successful_delete_result_contains_deleted_team_id(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user
    ):
        """The result object must carry the ID of the deleted team."""
        result = delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )
        assert result.deleted_team_id == "team-123"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Audit record
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestAuditRecord:
    """An audit record must be written on every successful deletion."""

    def test_audit_record_is_written_on_success(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user, audit_service
    ):
        """audit_service.record must be called exactly once on success."""
        delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )
        audit_service.record.assert_called_once()

    def test_audit_record_contains_actor_id(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user, audit_service
    ):
        """The audit entry must reference the acting user's ID."""
        delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )
        _, kwargs = audit_service.record.call_args
        audit_entry = kwargs.get("entry") or audit_service.record.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_entry.actor_id == system_admin_user.id

    def test_audit_record_contains_team_id(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user, audit_service
    ):
        """The audit entry must reference the deleted team's ID."""
        delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )
        _, kwargs = audit_service.record.call_args
        audit_entry = kwargs.get("entry") or audit_service.record.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_entry.resource_id == "team-123"

    def test_audit_record_action_is_delete_team(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user, audit_service
    ):
        """The audit entry must record the action as 'DELETE_TEAM'."""
        delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )
        _, kwargs = audit_service.record.call_args
        audit_entry = kwargs.get("entry") or audit_service.record.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_entry.action == "DELETE_TEAM"

    def test_audit_record_has_timestamp(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user, audit_service
    ):
        """The audit entry must carry a non-null timestamp."""
        delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )
        _, kwargs = audit_service.record.call_args
        audit_entry = kwargs.get("entry") or audit_service.record.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_entry.timestamp is not None

    def test_audit_record_not_written_on_auth_failure(
        self, delete_team_form_service, unauthenticated_user, audit_service
    ):
        """No audit record must be written when authentication fails."""
        delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=unauthenticated_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )
        audit_service.record.assert_not_called()

    def test_audit_record_not_written_on_authorisation_failure(
        self, delete_team_form_service, non_admin_user, audit_service
    ):
        """No audit record must be written when authorisation fails."""
        delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=non_admin_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )
        audit_service.record.assert_not_called()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Idempotency / edge cases
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestEdgeCases:
    """Guard against subtle failure modes."""

    def test_repository_failure_surfaces_as_error(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user, team_repository
    ):
        """If the repository raises, the service must return a failure result."""
        team_repository.delete.side_effect = RuntimeError("DB connection lost")

        result = delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )

        assert result.success is False
        assert result.error_code == "INTERNAL_ERROR"

    def test_repository_failure_does_not_write_audit_record(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user, team_repository, audit_service
    ):
        """When the repository raises, no audit record must be written."""
        team_repository.delete.side_effect = RuntimeError("DB connection lost")

        delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )

        audit_service.record.assert_not_called()

    def test_audit_failure_after_successful_delete_surfaces_as_error(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user, audit_service
    ):
        """
        If the audit write fails after a successful delete, the caller must be
        informed so the operation can be investigated.
        """
        audit_service.record.side_effect = RuntimeError("Audit store unavailable")

        result = delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )

        assert result.success is False
        assert result.error_code == "AUDIT_WRITE_FAILED"

    def test_delete_is_idempotent_on_repeated_calls_for_same_team(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user, team_repository
    ):
        """
        A second delete attempt for the same (now non-existent) team must
        return TEAM_NOT_FOUND rather than re-calling the repository delete.
        """
        # First call succeeds
        delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )

        # Simulate team no longer existing
        team_repository.find_by_id.return_value = None
        team_repository.exists.return_value = False

        # Second call
        result = delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD,
        )

        assert result.success is False
        assert result.error_code == "TEAM_NOT_FOUND"
        # Repository delete called only once (the first time)
        team_repository.delete.assert_called_once()

    def test_form_payload_with_extra_fields_is_still_valid(
        self, delete_team_form_service, system_admin_user
    ):
        """Unexpected extra fields in the payload must not break the operation."""
        payload = {**VALID_DELETE_PAYLOAD, "unknown_field": "surprise"}

        result = delete_team_form_service.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            payload=payload,
        )

        assert result.success is True
```

---

### Design notes

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| **Test doubles** | All collaborators (repository, audit service) are `MagicMock`. No real I/O is performed. |
| **Structure** | Six cohesive classes map directly to requirement sub-concerns: auth/z, mandatory-field validation, business rules, happy path, audit, and edge cases. |
| **Parametrize** | Used for blank-value and missing-field variants to keep individual tests DRY without hiding coverage. |
| **Audit assertions** | Tests inspect the `AuditEntry` object passed to the service — `actor_id`, `resource_id`, `action`, and `timestamp` are all verified independently. |
| **Negative audit tests** | Every failure mode asserts that `audit_service.record` was **not** called, preventing false-positive audit trails. |
| **Implementation contract** | The production code is expected at `app.services.team_service.DeleteTeamFormService`; the fixture import makes this explicit and easy to change. |
